_sources.discrepancies: Colorado is a universal vote-by-mail state — all registered voters automatically receive a mail ballot. NCSL classifies Colorado as non-photo ID (ID requested but signature verification on mail ballots is primary security mechanism). Colorado offers same-day voter registration at a voter service and polling center through Election Day. 2026 primary date estimated as June 30 per Colorado statute (last Tuesday in June); general election November 3. TODO verify exact 2026 cycle dates with Colorado SoS.

earlyVoting.notes: All voters receive a mail ballot automatically. Voter service and polling centers (VSPCs) open for in-person voting and same-day registration. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
All registered voters receive a mail ballot automatically by mid-October. Voter service and polling centers open for in-person voting starting approximately 15 days before Election Day.

Colorado is a vote-by-mail state. All registered voters automatically receive a mail ballot. Voter service and polling centers (VSPCs) open for in-person voting and ballot drop-off. Same-day registration available at VSPCs through Election Day.

acceptedIds: Colorado driver's license or ID card
Utility bill, bank statement, government check, or other government document with name and address
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Colorado allows use of phones and electronic devices at voting locations. Photographing completed ballots to share publicly is permitted (selfie law repealed). Do not photograph or record other voters without consent.
additionalRules: All registered voters automatically receive a mail ballot — no application required.
Colorado has a semi-closed primary: registered party voters vote in their party's primary; unaffiliated voters may request either party's primary ballot.
Same-day voter registration is available at any voter service and polling center (VSPC) through Election Day.
Ballots may be dropped off at any VSPC or official drop box in your county.

eligibility: All registered voters automatically receive a ballot by mail
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: No application required — ballots are automatically mailed to all registered voters
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by 7:00 p.m. on Election Day, or postmarked by Election Day and received within 8 days
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Drop off by 7 p.m. on Election Day or mail postmarked by Election Day (received within 8 days).
